title: Sales Development Representative (SDR/BDR)
setting: A technology company with a dedicated outbound sales development team
description: You're an SDR (Sales Development Representative) or BDR (Business Development Representative) at a tech company. Your entire job is to generate qualified meetings for the Account Executive team. You prospect, research, write outreach, make cold calls, and qualify inbound leads. It's high-activity, high-energy, and the best training ground in sales — most top AEs started as SDRs.
salary: $80,000-$120,000 AUD OTE. Base salary typically $60,000-$80,000 with $20,000-$40,000 in variable commission based on meetings booked and pipeline generated. Top SDRs who consistently exceed targets often get promoted to AE within 12-18 months.

schedule.activity: Start of day: check email and Slack for inbound leads that came in overnight. Respond within 5 minutes to any hot leads.
schedule.note: Speed to lead matters. Inbound leads responded to within 5 minutes are 10x more likely to convert.

schedule.activity: Daily standup: 10-minute team huddle. Share yesterday's wins, today's targets, and any blockers.
schedule.note: SDR teams are high-energy. Celebrate bookings, share what's working, and keep morale high.

schedule.activity: Prospect research block: research 10-15 new accounts, build personalised notes for outreach, find trigger events
schedule.note: Spend 5-8 minutes per account. Find a trigger event or personalisation hook for each one.

schedule.activity: Cold calling power hour: 25-40 dials targeting decision-makers at researched accounts
schedule.note: Smile when you dial. Energy comes through the phone. Aim for 3-5 conversations per hour.

schedule.activity: Email and LinkedIn outreach: send personalised emails and LinkedIn messages to prospects who didn't pick up
schedule.note: Multi-channel is key. The call warms them up for the email, and the email warms them up for the LinkedIn message.

schedule.activity: Inbound lead qualification: call 3 inbound leads, ask qualifying questions (BANT), and book meetings for AEs
schedule.note: Inbound leads are already interested — your job is to qualify quickly and book the right AE meeting.
Recharge. The afternoon calling block needs your best energy.

schedule.activity: Second calling block: 20-30 dials targeting different time zones or decision-makers who are harder to reach in the morning
schedule.note: C-suite executives are often more reachable between 1-3pm when their meeting schedule clears.

schedule.activity: Sequence management: enrol new prospects into automated sequences, pause sequences for prospects who responded, update CRM
schedule.note: Keep your sequences clean. Nothing's more embarrassing than an automated follow-up hitting someone who already booked a meeting.

schedule.activity: Coaching session: 30-minute 1:1 with your SDR manager. Review call recordings, discuss techniques, set goals.
schedule.note: Top SDRs seek feedback actively. Bring a specific call you want help with — don't wait for your manager to find issues.

schedule.activity: Creative outreach: record personalised Loom videos for top-priority prospects, engage with prospect LinkedIn content
schedule.note: Save creative outreach for your highest-value targets. A 45-second personalised video can cut through the noise.

schedule.activity: Follow-up block: call back prospects who asked you to call later, respond to email threads, handle objections
schedule.note: Most meetings are booked on follow-up, not first touch. Persistence wins.

schedule.activity: Admin and planning: update CRM with all activities, review tomorrow's target list, prepare notes for morning calls
schedule.note: End the day with a clean CRM and a prepared call list for tomorrow morning.

schedule.activity: Skill development: listen to a call recording, read a sales book, or practise a new cold call opener
schedule.note: The SDR role is a 12-18 month apprenticeship. The reps who invest in learning get promoted fastest.

responsibilities: Generate 15-25 qualified meetings per month for the Account Executive team
Make 50-80 outbound calls per day and send 30-50 personalised emails
Research target accounts and identify key decision-makers and trigger events
Qualify inbound leads using BANT or similar frameworks within SLA (typically <5 minutes)
Execute multi-channel outreach sequences across email, phone, and LinkedIn
Maintain accurate CRM data: every call logged, every email tracked, every lead updated
Collaborate with AEs to ensure smooth handoff of qualified meetings
Continuously improve through coaching, call reviews, and skill development
challenges: Extremely high rejection rate — expect 95%+ of calls and emails to be ignored or declined
Repetitive daily activity: the same call scripts, the same objections, the same rejections
Maintaining energy and positivity when you've had 30 'no's in a row
Hitting monthly targets that reset to zero on the 1st of every month
Getting past gatekeepers and voicemail walls to reach decision-makers
Working in a metrics-driven environment where every activity is tracked and measured
rewards: The fastest on-ramp into a high-paying sales career — most AE roles require SDR experience
Rapid skill development: you'll learn more in 12 months as an SDR than most people learn in 3 years
Clear promotion path: SDRs who hit target consistently get promoted to AE within 12-18 months
Competitive team environment with regular incentives, competitions, and celebrations
Building resilience and communication skills that are valuable in every career
The thrill of booking a meeting that turns into a six-figure deal for your AE partner
Earning potential grows rapidly: SDR to AE doubles your income within 1-2 years
careerPath: SDR/BDR (0-12 months) — master prospecting, cold outreach, and qualification
Senior SDR / SDR Team Lead (12-18 months) — mentor new SDRs, handle strategic accounts
Account Executive (18-24 months) — carry your own quota, run full sales cycles
Senior Account Executive (3-5 years) — larger deals, strategic selling
Sales Manager (5-7 years) — lead an SDR or AE team
Alternative paths: Sales Enablement, Sales Operations, Customer Success, Marketing — SDR skills transfer everywhere
